content.boundary: Applies to the statutory age and structure of first academic tracking nationwide; requires parliamentary legislative action; explicitly excludes higher-education admission and vocational-track content design except where sequencing requires touching the post-16 juncture
content.change_lever: Legislative amendment to Act CXC of 2011 converting 6- and 8-year gimnázium entry points into a unified comprehensive lower-secondary phase to age 14+, bundled with curriculum harmonization and anti-sorting catchment rules, following the Finnish/Polish (1999) bundled-reform template rather than an isolated age change
content.name: Structural delay of first tracking to age 14+ with unified comprehensive lower-secondary phase
content.system_problem: Hungary tracks 85% of students by academic performance versus a 43% OECD average, with the third-highest between-school variance in the OECD and 23.1% of performance variance explained by socioeconomic background versus 14.8% OECD average — outcomes structurally linked to early selection age rather than to any single admissions rule
